steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says Mrs. Sommers ‘becomes engrossed in the decision of how she should spend’ her money. The blank needs a quotation that shows deep, absorbed engagement with spending decisions specifically.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The question of investment was one that occupied her greatly...really absorbed in speculation and calculation.’: directly shows her engrossed in financial planning. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘The neighbors sometimes talked of certain ‘better days’...’: describes her past, not her engagement with spending decisions. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Mrs. Sommers was one who knew the value of bargains...’: describes a general shopping habit, not absorption in a specific spending decision. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It seemed to her a very large amount of money...’: describes her feeling about the sum, not her engagement with deciding how to spend it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
